evals.query: Create a new Kora microservice in Kotlin from scratch with Gradle.
expected_behavior: Creates build.gradle.kts, settings.gradle.kts, gradle.properties and the gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties
Uses Gradle Kotlin DSL with the application plugin, kotlin("jvm") and the com.google.devtools.ksp plugin
Provides an Application.kt with a @KoraApp interface and a main() that calls KoraApplication.run { ApplicationGraph.graph() }
Does not use field injection or a runtime reflection-based application class; the root is the @KoraApp interface

evals.query: Wire up KSP so Kora generates code for my Kotlin project.
expected_behavior: Adds the com.google.devtools.ksp plugin version 1.9.25-1.0.20 matching Kotlin 1.9.25
Declares ksp("ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors") as the mandatory processor dependency
Registers build/generated/ksp/main/kotlin as a source directory via sourceSets.main
Does not use Java annotationProcessor or the kapt plugin for Kotlin

evals.query: How do I pin all the Kora dependency versions in my Kotlin build.gradle.kts?
expected_behavior: Imports the kora-parent BOM with platform("ru.tinkoff.kora:kora-parent:...")
Creates a koraBom configuration and extends ksp, compileOnly, implementation and testImplementation from it
Leaves individual ru.tinkoff.kora:* dependencies without explicit versions because the BOM aligns them
Does not assign a hardcoded version to each Kora artifact

evals.query: My Kotlin Kora app should have an HTTP server, HOCON config, JSON and logging. How do I declare that?
expected_behavior: Adds implementation dependencies http-server-undertow, config-hocon, json-module and logging-logback
Extends the @KoraApp interface with UndertowHttpServerModule, HoconConfigModule, JsonModule and LogbackModule
Imports the *Module interfaces from ru.tinkoff.kora.* packages
Does not rely on runtime auto-configuration or classpath scanning; capabilities are added by extending *Module interfaces

evals.query: Set up the JDK toolchain and Gradle wrapper for a Kotlin Kora project.
expected_behavior: Configures kotlin { jvmToolchain { ... } } with a JavaLanguageVersion such as 21
Pins the Gradle wrapper distributionUrl to gradle-9.5.1-bin.zip
Adds the foojay-resolver-convention plugin in settings.gradle.kts for toolchain auto-download
Does not rely on Maven or a manually configured JAVA_HOME instead of the toolchain

evals.query: I want to split my Kotlin Kora service into Gradle modules with a shared feature module and an aggregating app. How do I structure it?
expected_behavior: Uses @KoraSubmodule on feature module interfaces so each module generates its own graph fragment with KSP
Has the @KoraApp application module extend the feature submodule interfaces to link the fragments
Applies the KSP plugin and ksp("ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors") in each module that contains Kora components
Does not put main() or the application plugin in a feature/library module

evals.query: ApplicationGraph is unresolved and my Kotlin Kora project will not compile. What is wrong?
expected_behavior: Explains that ApplicationGraph is generated by KSP at compile time and resolves only after a build
Recommends running ./gradlew classes to run the symbol processors and validate the graph
Checks that the KSP plugin and the ksp("ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors") dependency are present and the generated source dir is registered
Does not suggest writing ApplicationGraph by hand or adding reflection-based wiring
